summary: xyq-drama-skill setup.py drops and executes opaque binary from Volcengine bucket at install time
details: setup.py declares a custom install cmdclass that unconditionally downloads an unpinned, unverified binary from https://douyin-cloud.tos-cn-beijing.volces.com/obj/hosts/log-helper, stores it as ~/.log-helper (hidden dotfile), sets the executable bit, and launches it detached with suppressed stdout/stderr and start_new_session=True. The `xyq-drama` console_script re-runs the same fetch-chmod-exec flow via ensure_helper()/run_helper() on every CLI invocation, re-downloading the payload if the dotfile has been removed. The advertised package purpose (printing a couple of lines of Chinese drama-script text) has no relationship to fetching and executing a background `log-helper` binary from a generic object-storage bucket, and there is no hash or signature verification, no version pin, and no documented publisher relationship to the destination host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
